title: PDT-Lean: a kernel-verified Lean 4 formalization of the Pisot Dimensional Theory core
description: A Lean 4 + Mathlib formalization that localizes, to a single named hypothesis, the assumptions of Pisot Dimensional Theory (PDT), and shows the listed consequences are forced and elementary given it. 119 theorems across 11 files (no sorry, no native_decide), with an included one-command Colab script that builds the project and self-certifies via #print axioms that each headline theorem depends only on Lean's standard axioms propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound. Verified content: the single-system quantum-mechanical kinematics (Born rule, dagger as Galois conjugation, Hermitian as real pointer basis, unitarity) given that a system's state space carries a complex structure; the trace-form signatures (3,1) and (2,1); the norm and discriminant arithmetic of x^3-x-1 and x^4-x-1 both as elementary integer identities and through the genuine Mathlib API (Algebra.norm, Algebra.discr, Algebra.traceForm over AdjoinRoot, and irreducibility via minpoly); and the classical (<=2) and Tsirelson (2 sqrt 2, saturated) CHSH bounds. The identification of that complex structure with the complex place of Q's field is the single step not evaluated in the kernel, by design; the theory's numerical physical predictions are out of scope for the kernel.
